notes:
Two visible-in-the-app fixes: revert v1.0.49's broken vector icon back to v1.0.48's working raster pipeline (slightly tuned unsharp on small frames), and redesign the Connections > Software Update card so it actually reads as a software-update section.

### Icon: revert v1.0.49 vector pipeline, return to v1.0.48 raster + tuned unsharp

v1.0.49 attempted to render the icon from a hand-authored SVG via `resvg-py` to get vector-quality rasterization at every Windows DPI size (matching how Settings / Photos / Edge are rendered). It looked broken — the simplified SVG geometry (three flat horizontal bars + two small fold trapezoids) didn't carry the folded-S read at any size; even at 256 px the silhouette read as "stack of green bars," not a folded ribbon S. Without the v1.0.36 SVG's bright phosphor highlight strips (which we'd intentionally dropped because they collapse to a one-row antialiasing artifact at 16 px), the geometry alone couldn't communicate the S.

Reverted `scripts/regen_icon.py` to v1.0.48's PIL Lanczos pipeline:
- Source: `static/branding/wordmark-on-dark@3x.png` (1200×426). The Copilot-source artwork has the correct geometry — chevron tips, fold-shadow detail, the actual 3D fold cues that the simplified SVG was missing.
- Isolate the green S via green-channel dominance (G ≥ 60 AND G−R ≥ 40 AND G−B ≥ 40), 1 px erosion, 0.6 px sub-pixel blur on the mask edge.
- 10% padding so the glyph never touches the icon's outer pixel ring at 16 px.
- Multi-step Lanczos halving until within 2× of target, then a final Lanczos to the exact size. Each halving integrates 2×2 input pixels with the full kernel, beating one big resize for thin-edge artwork.

The one improvement on top of v1.0.48: stronger `UnsharpMask` on the ≤32 px frames (`radius=0.8, percent=130, threshold=1`, was `radius=0.6/percent=110/threshold=2` at ≤24 px in v1.0.48). Threshold 1 lets the unsharp affect near-flat regions too, so the S's gradient face panels get a subtle face-edge crispening rather than just edge-only sharpening; threshold 1 → percent 130 is the maximum tuning before halo artifacts appear at the corners. Frames 33-48 px keep v1.0.48's `radius=0.8, percent=90, threshold=2` because those already read fine and pushing the unsharp introduced visible haloing at the fold corners.

`branding/svg/asa-glyph-s.svg` is left in place as a brand reference (correct geometry concept, ASA-current colors), just no longer used as the icon source. `scripts/icon-compare/` PNGs are also kept as historical visual-comparison data.

### Connections page: Software Update card redesign

The card under Connections > Software Update used to look like:
- Header: a status-aware string that toggled between "ASA Terminal" (default), "ASA Terminal v1.0.49" (network error), and "Update available" (newer version detected). The header was doing two jobs (section heading AND live state), so it read confusingly — users saw "ASA Terminal" as the app name, not as the card's section title.
- Body: an inner div with its own `padding: var(--sp-4)` on top of the card's own `padding: var(--sp-5)` — doubled-up padding made the card feel half-empty.
- A faint version chip ("v1.0.49") in the corner of the header, easy to miss.
- A horizontal row at the bottom: status text on the left, "Check now" button on the right — the button looked like a control associated with the row, not with the page.

v1.0.50 redesign, no new colors, just cleaner hierarchy:
- `card__header` is now a STATIC "Software Update" section title (uses the existing `.card__header` small-caps style — small font, 1.5 letter-spacing, uppercase, `tx-3` color — same as every other section header in the dashboard).
- A small color-coded `bridge-update-state` pill on the right of the header carries the live state: green "✓ Up to date" / gold "Update available" / blank when network unknown. Same color tokens (`--gn`, `--gd`, `--tx-3`) the rest of the dashboard uses.
- Inner padding div removed — only the card's own `var(--sp-5)` (20 px) padding remains.
- Version is now the visual anchor: `font-mono`, 22 px, weight 700, `tx-0` color. Sits at the top of the body as a `v1.0.50`-style monospaced number. Status text (e.g. "v1.0.50 is ready — ...") sits directly under it as secondary copy.
- Auto-update toggle moved into a subtle inset row (1 px border, `bg-1` background, `radius-sm`) with a "Hourly background check" hint on the right — clearly a setting, not a status indicator.
- "Check for updates" button is now full-width and the bottom-most element, making it the unambiguous primary action of the card.

JS (`bridgeRefreshUpdateStatus` / `bridgeCheckUpdates`) updated to write to the new `bridge-update-state` element instead of the now-removed `bridge-update-title`. Button-text reset on completion changed from "Check now" to "Check for updates" to match the new label.

### Files touched

- `scripts/regen_icon.py` — reverted to PIL Lanczos pipeline with v1.0.50-tuned unsharp on ≤32 px frames
- `tray-app/src-tauri/icons/{32x32,64x64,128x128,128x128@2x,icon}.png`, `tray-app/src-tauri/icons/icon.ico` — regenerated (back to clean folded-S rendering)
- `dashboard.html` — Software Update card redesigned (markup + JS in `bridgeRefreshUpdateStatus` / `bridgeCheckUpdates`)

### Auto-updater note

Same pipeline as v1.0.49 — `release.ps1`'s `Get-InstallerUrl` already rewrites spaces → dots for the manifest URL, UTF-8 explicit on CHANGELOG read, BOM-free on `latest.json` write, and `cmd.exe`-shielded `git clone`/`commit`/`push` in the manifest-push loop. No `release.ps1` changes in v1.0.50.
